original_image_tags: The back of a yellowed, aged document page. The paper has two punch holes at the top and shows signs of wear, including torn corners and edges. The page is largely blank, showing only faded, reverse text from the other side. Multiple blue ink reception stamps from December 1966 are visible, indicating routing through FBI and other intelligence divisions.

elements.description: Multiple overlapping blue ink date stamps, including 'DEC 22 1 02 PM 66 REC'D SULLIVAN FBI JUSTICE', 'DEC 22 4 42 PM 66 REC'D INTELL DIV.', 'DEC 21 5 30 PM 66 F.B.I. RECEIVED-DIRECTOR', and 'DEC 21 8 51 AM 66 REC'D DE LACI FBI'. Faint red handwritten text or initials are also visible.

judge.reason: Both descriptions agree on the core visual content of blue ink reception stamps from December 1966 on the back of an aged document, but Description B provides significantly more specific quoted stamp text that Description A does not detail, creating no direct contradiction on the visual elements.
